title: Colin Runs the Red Mountain 50k
summary:
After seeing people racing the previous week, I was very motivated to do a race myself. Luckily, there was a 50k happening in our general vicinity with spots still available, the Red Mountain 50k. We adjusted our schedule slightly, and I signed up for the race. Over the past few months, I had been training for the Canyons 100k in April, and wanted to use this race to gauge my fitness before the bigger event. I had not had this much dedicated training in my previous ultramarathons and was excited to see how fast I was able to go. The race consisted of four unique loops around different streches of the trail system outside town. Coincidentally, I had biked a few of the trails in past trips to the St. George area, so I was relatively familiar with the terrain. The first 20 miles went very smooth. None of the trails were too steep or technical, so I was able to push myself pretty hard the entire time. Just before the final aid station around mile 23, I took a pretty big spill when I tripped on the rock. Luckily, I escaped with just a few scrapes and bruises. Shortly after, I briefly saw Helen and Maya, which gave me a nice boost of motivation. Unfortunately, the motivation was short lived, and I slowed to a power hike for the next few miles up the final climb of the race. After cresting the final hill, the allure of the finish line was enough to make me run the final miles. I ended up finishing in 4:16 and got 6th place overall. I was very happy with the race result and afterwards, Helen and I celebrated by getting ice cream from the local Nielson's Frozen Custard shop.

-Colin

photos.caption: Our campsite the night before the race.
We had a little ranch dressing spill that required some major cleanup.
Colin after finishing the Red Mountain 50k.
